meaning: adj.阿波罗神的,阿波罗崇拜的,具有古典美的
examples: He says that we have separated two important aspects of ourselves: the "Dionysian" and the "Apollonian".
Passing Boreen Point, home to the historic Apollonian Hotel, famed for its Sunday spit roast, we see stingrays and stonefish in waist-deep Lake Cootharaba.
The Apollonian, by contrast, represents the power to deal with the powerful surge of vital energy, to harness destructive powers, and to transmute these into creative act.
"The road of excess leads to the palate of wisdom, " he jokes, paraphrasing Blake, though his temperament seems to me a little more Apollonian than Dionysian.
